summary: This notebook provides a reproducible baseline pipeline for the Child Mind Institute competition, combining clinical/physical tabular data with wearable actigraphy time-series data. It demonstrates a complete workflow from data loading and imputation to custom feature engineering, threshold optimization for Quadratic Weighted Kappa, and multi-model ensembling. The author emphasizes fixing random seeds across libraries to ensure stable leaderboard scores. Readers should learn how to handle mixed data types, compress time-series inputs, and optimize discrete thresholds for ordinal regression tasks.
competition_flow: Loads tabular and actigraphy time-series data, imputes missing values, engineers interaction and autoencoder-derived features, trains gradient boosting and TabNet models via 5-fold stratified CV, optimizes prediction thresholds for QWK, and combines outputs via voting regressors and majority vote to generate a final submission.
data_reading: Reads CSV files using Polars and Pandas.
Loads time-series parquet files via os.listdir and pd.read_parquet with parallel processing.
data_processing: Filters out rows with missing target values before supervised learning.
Replaces infinities with NaN and imputes missing numeric data using KNNImputer and SimpleImputer.
Casts categorical season columns to Enum/category, fills missing categories with 'Missing', and converts them to integers via custom label mappings.
features_engineering: Computes statistical summaries of actigraphy time-series data.
Compresses time-series into latent features using a custom PyTorch AutoEncoder.
Creates domain-derived interaction and ratio features (e.g., BMI_Age, Internet_Hours_Age, BFP_BMI, Muscle_to_Fat).
loss_functions: Mean Squared Error (MSE) used as the default loss and explicit eval_metric for TabNet.
Quadratic Weighted Kappa used as the optimization objective for threshold tuning via scipy.optimize.minimize.
ensembling: Combines predictions from multiple regressors using sklearn's VotingRegressor with weighted averages, and applies majority voting across three separate submission pipelines to produce the final output.
insights: Explicitly fixing random seeds across PyTorch, LightGBM, and OS environments is critical for reproducible leaderboard scores.
Threshold optimization on out-of-fold predictions can significantly boost ordinal regression metrics like QWK.
Compressing high-dimensional time-series data via autoencoders or simple statistics effectively augments tabular models.
critical_findings: The target variable distribution is highly imbalanced, with approximately half the samples in class 0 and very few in class 3.
Some features present in the training set are missing in the test set, requiring careful column alignment.
Supervised learning requires filtering out samples with missing target values before processing.
